name: Josh Hicks
subject: Our neighbors are counting on us
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
body:
I know this is an anxious time, friends.
Our schools and businesses are closed, and we’re being asked to stay home — away from our family and friends. We’re all looking for connection right now, and there’s never been a more important time to take care of our neighbors.
Many of our neighbors in this district face food insecurity. That’s why my family has always been proud to support God’s Pantry.
Now, in the middle of a public health and economic crisis, our community members are counting on God’s Pantry, and us, more than ever.
So, I’m asking you to join me in supporting God’s Pantry and their work during this pandemic. [https://godspantry.org/donate/covid19/] The best way to support them is with a financial contribution — they support more than 400 food banks and meal programs in Central and Eastern Kentucky.
Our community is strong, and I know that we’ll get through this by taking care of each other.
Thank you,
Josh
Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
Josh Hicks for Congress
PO Box 1214
Lexington, KY 40588
United States
Josh Hicks is a Democrat running to represent Kentucky’s 6th Congressional District. He will be a representative who puts the needs of his constituents front and center in Washington. Josh grew up in Fleming County before leaving to serve in the Marines. After two deployments, he returned to Kentucky to serve his community as a police officer and attorney.
He’s running to represent hardworking, everyday Kentuckians. Not corporations. That means his campaign is relying on grassroots donations — lots of small donations from emails like this — not big checks from corporate special interests.

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
We know that Charles has the strongest message to defeat Mitch and turn Kentucky blue — but if our campaign falls short on this fundraising deadline our momentum can crumble.
#
In just 9 days, we will be facing our last FEC end-of-quarter fundraising deadline before the primary.
**Here’s why this deadline is such a big deal for our campaign:**
1. Everyone in the media and the establishment will be watching how much our grassroots campaign is able to raise and will judge the strength of our progressive movement by that amount.
2. How much we raise by March 31st will ultimately determine how many resources our campaign will have to organize, broadcast Charles’ message, and reach out to voters.

We know that Charles has the strongest message to defeat Mitch and turn Kentucky blue — but if our campaign falls short on this fundraising deadline our momentum can crumble.
[**Will you help us send a message to our opponents & the establishment that this progressive movement is on the rise? Donating even just $5 makes a huge difference.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16383567/173959440/-1367995535?refcode=em20200322&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGYxMmY1My02MTZjLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sX82zTF0Key1aa1I4iovOHizv2hSEI0oKO31imVYsgU=)
****[Contribute $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16383568/173959441/-1367995535?refcode=em20200322&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGYxMmY1My02MTZjLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sX82zTF0Key1aa1I4iovOHizv2hSEI0oKO31imVYsgU=)****
****
**We’re proud to be a people-powered campaign. But to be frank with you,**it’s not easy.** **
**Not accepting donations from special interests, corporate PACs, or the fossil fuel industry puts us at a huge financial disadvantage in comparison to our opponents. **
**It means we have to work two times (if not three times) as hard as other campaigns to raise the resources we need to be competitive in this race. It also means we’re really relying on donations from emails like this to stay on pace with our fundraising goals. **
****But we know it’s the necessary choice if we want to create a new kind of politics that’s built by people and not beholden to the dark money that’s influenced our leaders for too long. ****
**[**It would mean so much to us if you can chip in even $5 to our campaign today and help show the country the power of grassroots movement. Team Booker**

name: Tina Smith
subject: Paid sick leave
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
Folks, here’s the truth: Millions of Americans still don't have access to any paid sick leave. Not only is that morally wrong, but it puts all of us at risk — especially during the coronavirus pandemic. **One important way we can support our workers and our communities is to guarantee universal paid sick leave to all workers.** Far too many folks can’t afford to lose a paycheck because it could mean falling into debt or not being able to put food on the table. But it doesn’t have to be this way. We can and must do better. That’s why my Senate colleagues and I want to provide clear protections for workers — not just during this public health emergency, but always. **[If you believe we must stand with our workers in sickness and in health, add your name to demand Congress pass universal paid sick leave now. name: Nick Clemons, Team Kennedy
subject: Hope you are holding up well.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kennedy for Massachusetts
body:
| [](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2140/3454439107/VEsH/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "http://www.kennedyforma.com/")
---

| | Hi, This has been a really difficult week for all of us. As I am sure it has been difficult for you and your family. But know, even though we temporarily suspended all political campaign activity during this epidemic, we are still fighting each and every day for this community and the people of Massachusetts. Here are some things we've done that we're extremely proud of: **This week, the [Hill reported that](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2142/3454439107/VEsE/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.boston.com/news/politics/2020/03/17/joe-kennedy-coronavirus-fundraising"): _"Joe Kennedy III's campaign response to the virus has been one of the more unique ones so far."_ ** So, what have we been doing exactly? **1\. Joe is hosting virtual, multi-platform town halls.** Every weeknight, Joe hosted a special guest live to discuss a critical element of COVID-19 response. On Thursday he hosted Andy Slavitt, who ran the Affordable Care Act rollout for President Obama. Wednesday he hosted **[Jos e Andres](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e214c/3454439107/VEsF/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://twitter.com/joekennedy/status/1240428006335606784")**, chef and founder of World Central Kitchen, to discuss ensuring those most in need across the country have food. Before that he was joined by Jason Furman, the former Chair of President Obama's Economic Council, and State Rep. Jon Santiago, an emergency room physician at Boston Medical Center. Make sure you follow Joe on [**Facebook**](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e214d/3454439107/VEsC/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.facebook.com/JoeKennedyIII/") and [**Twitter**](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e214e/3454439107/VEsD/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://twitter.com/joekennedy") to watch our next town hall today at 4 p.m. with health care champion and patient advocate, Peter Morley. **2\. We created an [online response center](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2149/3454439107/VEsA/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/covid19/") as well as a ["virtual office"](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e214b/3454439107/VEsB/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.facebook.com/groups/KennedyForMA/") on Facebook, where people can access the latest information about COVID-19 and Joe's efforts. ** We are continuing to keep this updated as things evolve. **[Please visit](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2149/3454439107/VEsO/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/covid19/")** the website for regular updates. You can also join our **[virtual office](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2155/3454439107/VEsP/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.facebook.com/groups/KennedyForMA/?ref=pages_groups_card&source_id=309993142375589") **here. **3\. Joe is checking in on Facebook with a live family video every morning.** You may have caught the **[Kennedy Chorus sing along](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2157/3454439107/VEsHBQ/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.facebook.com/JoeKennedyIII/videos/430035827808047/")****** on St. Patrick's Day morning. And you don't want to miss his daughter Ellie's rising star! Be on the lookout for these on Joe's Facebook in the morning - they're a little bit of light-heartedness in really tough times. **4\. Instead of raising money for his campaign, Joe has asked supporters to [donate to the social service organizations](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2142/3454439107/VEsHBA/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.boston.com/news/politics/2020/03/17/joe-kennedy-coronavirus-fundraising") on the front lines of this crisis.** You have probably received a series of emails from Joe, asking you to consider chipping in to donate to organizations like Project Bread, Pine Street Inn, and Meals on Wheels -- groups doing everything they can to help those most in need, from seniors to food-insecure families. Thanks to your incredible generosity -- particularly during such anxious economic times -- we have raised thousands of dollars to date and intend to continue this effort in the weeks ahead. [**If you'd like to chip in a few dollars, please **](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2151/3454439107/VEsHBw/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-covidma?refcode=2020-03-22-covidupdate&recurring=1&amount=7")**[click here.](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2153/3454439107/VEsHBg/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-covidma?refcode=2020-03-16-charities-mao&recurring=1&amount=7")** **5\. Joe is consistently reaching out to Spanish-language speakers who have reported trouble accessing COVID-19 resources and information.** From using Facebook to communicate the **[latest information and guidance](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e215d/3454439107/VEsHAQ/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.facebook.com/JoeKennedyIII/videos/1040429729656870/")** to ensuring Spanish language materials on our online response center, Joe has been using his Spanish skills to communicate consistently with the Latinx community in MA and across the country. Check out his **[great interview with El Mundo.](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e215e/3454439107/VEsHAA/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.facebook.com/JoeKennedyIII/posts/3582510175123853")** **6\. Joe has been utilizing local press across Massachusetts to deliver needed public service announcements and important updates on Congressional response efforts.** As you know, being accessible to everyone in the state has always been a priority for Joe. To the extent he is able, he has put a premium on connecting with local media outlets North, South, and West to make sure every constituent has access to the latest information. A few great examples here: **WPRI: [Kennedy: Congress should send Americans cash to boost economy](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2158/3454439107/VEsHAw/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.wpri.com/health/coronavirus/kennedy-congress-should-send-americans-cash-to-boost-economy/")** **Fall River Herald News: [Kennedy: 'We have to make sure people don't lose everything'](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2159/3454439107/VEsHAg/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.heraldnews.com/news/20200317/kennedy-we-have-to-make-sure-people-dont-lose-everything")** **Attleboro Sun Chronicle: [Kennedy fields calls about coronavirus pandemic at Attleboro district office](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e215a/3454439107/VEsHDQ/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.thesunchronicle.com/news/coronavirus/kennedy-fields-calls-about-coronavirus-pandemic-at-attleboro-district-office/article_b40367a6-d11c-59de-8239-fc94c0f3b683.html")** **WATD : [Marshfield: Congressman Joe Kennedy III Discusses Coronavirus with WATD](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e215b/3454439107/VEsHDA/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://959watd.com/blog/2020/03/marshfield-congressman-joe-kennedy-iii-discusses-coronavirus/")** **7\. Joe is leading the charge for an aggressive, progressive policy response to this crisis.** His Congressional office has been working around the clock on legislative response efforts -- from access to personal protective equipment, to increased Medicaid funds, paid sick leave, and a host of other policy proposals. Most notably, Joe rolled out his own **[worker's economic recovery package](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2164/3454439107/VEsEBQ/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.wgbh.org/news/national-news/2020/03/17/rep-joe-kennedy-releases-plan-for-cash-payments-to-americans-struggling-in-wake-of-covid-19") **that contains an ambitious direct cash assistance plan for every American family, as well as expanded unemployment insurance and increased funding for medical centers across the country. **8\. Joe is [personally calling constituents](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e215a/3454439107/VEsEBA/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.thesunchronicle.com/news/coronavirus/kennedy-fields-calls-about-coronavirus-pandemic-at-attleboro-district-office/article_b40367a6-d11c-59de-8239-fc94c0f3b683.html") and hosting regular Zoom calls with constituency groups (students, faith leaders, veterans) to check in on them and hear what their communities need.** When he's not doing any of the things mentioned above, Joe is on the phone -- calling local Mayors, state officials, Congressional colleagues, frontline health care workers across the state, and countless others battling this crisis. He is doing intimate conversations with individual constituency groups via Zoom - including a town hall with college students where he discussed unique challenges they face from displacement to hunger to loans. Those are just a few of the things we did this week. We are so proud of this community for being strong and have been so impressed by the outpouring of support we have received online from folks like you. Talk soon, stay safe, stay inside, and wash your hands. We'll get through this, together. \- Nick _Nick Clemons
Campaign Manager_ __ |

PO Box 15, Boston, MA 02137.

[**Contact us here**](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900f0/55b66746/23c8d3b9/80e2161/3454439107/VEsEAw/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/contact/"). |

name: Laura Loomer
subject: What can you do right now?
body:
None of us has ever experienced anything like the Coronavirus Pandemic in our lifetime.
| | | [](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-368597-866266-4922255-e9d2e5bbe8-f1b09057c2)
Patriot,

None of us has ever experienced anything like the Coronavirus Pandemic in our lifetime.

But, we have experienced trying times that bring us together. From 9/11 to local tragedies, Americans are known to step up and rise to the occasion when we need it the most.

[](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-368597-866266-4922254-9654229654-f1b09057c2) In the coming days and weeks, a group of Americans are going to need our help the most.

On Friday, I organized ten of my campaign volunteers to donate blood at a local blood bank. You can safely do the same.

As healthcare professionals are overwhelmed with COVID-19, other Americans need our help and the easiest way to assist is by donating blood.

[Just click here to find a blood bank near you](https://em.lauraloomerforcongress.com/ga/click/2-561017072-4395-368597-866266-4922254-9654229654-f1b09057c2).

As my blood was drawn, First Lady Melania Trump announced that blood banks around the nation are running low on blood, and she called on healthy Americans to donate as soon as possible.

With the majority of our population in isolation and quarantine, the situation regarding the American blood supply gets worse daily.

The Coronavirus is NOT transmittable through blood, so as long as you’re healthy you can call ahead and schedule a time to avoid any crowds.

And take other healthy household members along with you to make the most of the trip.

Even though we are not door-knocking or petitioning currently for the sake of social distancing, my campaign volunteers are ready to be mobilized on a moment's notice to donate blood and do anything else that is needed to assist our community here in Florida's 21st Congressional District.

Some of my volunteers are hand-sewing CDC-compliant masks which they plan to donate to local health workers.

Thanks again for all that you do and stay tuned for more ways to help others as we work to get through this tough time that is impacting all of us.

Respectfully,

Laura Loomer

P.S. If you live in Florida's 21st Congressional District, make sure to visit my campaign site where we are posting updates about how COVID-19 is impacting Floridians.

Paid for by Laura Loomer for Congress Inc Contributions to Laura Loomer for Congress Inc are not tax deductible for federal income tax purposes. Contributions from corporations, labor unions, federal contractors, and foreign nationals are prohibited.
---

LAKE WORTH, FL. 33460

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: My humble suggestions
body:
Peter,
If your inbox is like mine, it’s littered with emails from every company and organization telling you what they are doing to protect their employees and customers from COVID-19.
We’re grateful for everyone’s efforts because we know it's our elderly and people with underlying medical conditions who are most at risk. I’m especially thankful our President is taking bold measures to keep everyone safe.
I’m guessing by now you are religious about washing your hands, not touching your face, disinfecting all hard surfaces, and you’re mastering social distancing, am I right?
**Beyond all the instructions and using common sense, there is a far more serious lesson for the American people to glean out of this situation. As fear has filled the hearts of Americans across the country it’s revealed a lack of faith.**
Instead of turning to God and trusting in Him, people everywhere are panicking, believing their destiny is in their own hands.
I said on my TBN show this past weekend, while I will take all the precautions I can, I’m not going to live my life out of fear of what might happen. I know God numbers my days, and there is nothing I or anybody else can do to add to them (Job 14:5).
As long as God has us here on this earth, we are to be good stewards with all the blessings He has given us! Jeremiah 29:7 says, “Work for the peace and prosperity of the city where I sent you into exile. Pray to the Lord for it, for its welfare will determine your welfare.”
**So, here are my humble suggestions:**
1. **Follow**[**CDC guidelines**](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=7Ho65PobbrrlpD_Ow9Pq_3y6Rl1ZYx2Sap7xXzPRZTClygurCXHSMHn1KGvHSr9EGqL_Kt0cOMPKBl2Eb1AdwkfSTmhsY2x4c4FDSaq_Ntc&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200322apl01nd&n=1) to avoid spreading the virus and use common sense.
2. **Pray faithfully for our country**. Jason and the team at My Faith Votes are asking everyone to pray daily for 30 days. They have created this free [My Faith Votes six point prayer guide](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iVl2bNHZpIGlNoQAduC0VVdQwUylQtNErWARJjql45DSJvJTIGXsJR_PPsFAZMg1cQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200322apl01nd&n=2).
3. **Check in on the elderly and those who need assistance**. Give them a call, bring groceries, medications or meet a need for them.
4. **Don’t forget the November Elections!** While events have all been canceled, the November 2, 2020, election is still on the calendar. If we want Christians to turn out and protect our most sacred freedoms, we can’t take a coronavirus break from our efforts, and [we still need your support to make it happen](https://secured.myfaithvotes.org/p200322apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200322apl01nd&n=3).

If we want to keep faith at the heart of America, it’s up to you and me and Christians across the nation.
Remember, answer fear with faith. We won’t be rocked by the coronavirus or anything else. We will stay our course because our God does not change, our responsibility remains, and the work before us is far from over.
Thank you for working with us to awaken Christians to bring their faith forward amid the coronavirus, in the November elections, and every opportunity in-between.
**United. We Stand.**
Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200322apl01nd&n=4)
©2020 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com).
To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/unsubscribe?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200322apl01nd&n=5).

name: Betsy
subject: Stay Safe and Up to Date
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Betsy Rader
body:
| |
---
Peter, For months now, I have been in living rooms, back yards, coffee shops, and union halls, talking face-to-face with the people of District 18. **That 's all rapidly changed and I know that we are all doing what we can to help during this time.** For some, that means staying in place, homeschooling children, or performing jobs that must be done. For me, I've been able to [donate blood](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/LSa1tHe7faOJS7sfhYMf2KJL0-2N_bE2vIlAy19SipA=), volunteer at the [Greater Cleveland Food Bank](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/HKHbeZLlABJwWDr9SbD9WqJiaaEfU5_TL6q5dn1eCOI=), and [give interviews to the newspapers ](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/d3ndvIjCVhNgZmbNRQK3bCCJTUSLtQjQPYLBbvwIzAY=?fbclid=IwAR2tVVtWtXMr7G9CklaZE-u4yUBM8FiqkYNqMtjk2FzPUR5Gq8Zv8ldddaA)about topics like workplace safety. I've been on social media doing [interviews with expert physicians](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/BLgzhckBmFuF0XMOdsYRXSlblKpYbQShqu9uXJWsFaM=), as well as discussing [new unemployment and sick leave laws.](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/JZNkKRs02teyCENOpIy0njuymyT9Uo2ayjc-lvPzfkA=) **_Right now, it 's essential to support our community by following our Governor's [stay at home order ](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/8TFQz-fxPxG5u1txSLtCLmPaCdd0SatJu_4eWVWDIkY=)and doing our best to stay apart and stay healthy -- to protect ourselves and others._** So you will be seeing some changes from our campaign, including more [_Talks with Docs_](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/Gc547wK4isDt8A4Xw65S8P4RjNxOF4bsb_tKzrk6b8Q=)to hear from experts about the current pandemic, virtual events to engage with volunteers and voters, and more. We're postponing our house parties and face-to-face voter outreach, but we'll still be running an active grassroots campaign because ensuring a strong democracy continues to be important work. **Things are changing, but what isn 't changing is my dedication to the community and especially to those who are most vulnerable in crises like these. _This pandemic shows more than ever the need for good people in government who believe in science,_ and I hope you'll continue to stay engaged and support us as I continue to work to be the next Senator of District 18.** On the right column are things you can do right now, and please know my thoughts are with you during these difficult days. Thank you for your continued support. Take care, | _Things you can do right now!_ __ Keep checking the [CDC website](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/uPgoxUfRD7XM7zPkBymTYLM3x1bkwtccYachuwHTlbU=) for updates on what you can do to prevent the spread and stay healthy Stay engaged with the campaign by [liking us & sharing content on Facebook](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/V0Hun_w5cd_mC5iyimUYqrC11qh27vNNfjJO4cAOSKQ=) and [following us on Twitter](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/KYTl27TyeJ5wq81ecRpjLZ7IgxXmak3rtRvAT4FN2UE=) [Sign up on our website](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/W3c-2xQ6-UcZ0NRduWGLz79ELpYewi-mucPvBSU1YrE=) to stay informed of upcoming virtual events, issues facing District 18, and more. [E-mail us](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/qbXqM4TYX_JKzzpW6w3BeA795sKjBdUx0OVlNlMuUfI=?subject=Staying%20involved) to let us know your ideas for staying involved while staying inside. Support the campaign with a donation, as we continue to stay safe and healthy, while still fighting for the people who deserve good representation in Columbus! [](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/mXNbl7bRUfhwYA8GIdvpnN0A87tNvQMKQ61IBLHMht0=?refcode=c19-1) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/9PwVv572glo_wqjbR_pPbojGU7wL3-OH0cPOO6x_uyE=/LSa1tHe7faOJS7sfhYMf2KJL0-2N_bE2vIlAy19SipA=)

name: Tom Steyer
subject: An update
body:
| | | |
---
Hello from San Francisco — In the three weeks since suspending my presidential campaign, I’ve been grateful to spend quality time with Kat and my kids and catch up after the whirlwind of the campaign trail. I’ve also been thinking about how to continue fighting for the people, while watching the world, our country, and our home state of California grapple with the coronavirus pandemic. If you’re feeling uncertain, concerned about your health and that of your loved ones, and worried for our shared future — know that you’re not alone. But even during a public health emergency affecting the entire globe, life goes on: Communities like Denmark, South Carolina and Flint, Michigan are still fighting for clean drinking water. Our planet is still facing the existential threat of climate change. The Trump administration is still prioritizing corporations over people in need. I don’t know for sure what’s next for me. But like I said when I started my campaign, and again when I ended it, my heart is in these issues — win, lose, or draw,**I am in this fight.** I will keep you posted as things evolve, and in the meantime, I’ll keep reaching out about the fights we can and must win together. Stay tuned and take care,
---
---
Tom Steyer

Whether you are calling your elderly neighbors or ordering takeout from your favorite local restaurant, we want to hear about how you are supporting your community.

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: Spread some hope and compassion today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
Hello --

This public health emergency our country is grappling with is simply unprecedented -- and it requires all of us to step up for one another, especially our most vulnerable friends and neighbors.

One of the best ways you can make a difference is by making a donation to frontline organizations addressing the critical, emergency needs of our fellow citizens during this crisis.

The Food Bank of the Rockies is working overtime to distribute food and supplies to those most in need. Food insecurity is going to be a major problem in the coming weeks, especially for our elderly or those who have lost their jobs or income source this month.

With the worst of this crisis still ahead of us, anything you can do today to support those in need will make a huge difference. So, I'm asking right now to chip in what you can here:

If you are financially able, please make a contribution to the Food Bank of the Rockies. Your donation will go to immediately ensure people in our community have the food they need during this pandemic.

secure.actblue.com/donate/jh-covid19?refcode=em200322

If we're going to spread anything during this tough moment in time, it's going to be hope and compassion, not fear. Thanks again for stepping up today and for being a part of our team.

Stay safe,

-- John-------

name: Christy Smith
subject: Some COVID-19 resources for Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | Peter,

I hope you are staying safe and healthy during this time. I want to share some resources and information about coronavirus -- from symptoms to ways to mitigate the spread of the disease, this is all good to know while we are all working on getting through this.
**
### 1\. IF YOU LIVE IN CALIFORNIA
** Governor Gavin Newsom has called for all Californians to remain indoors except for absolutely necessary reasons like a trip to the pharmacy, a visit to a doctor, or a run to the grocery store to stock up on food.

Folks are safer at home, and by practicing social distancing, we can mitigate the spread of coronavirus. Projections show that more than 50% of Californians could contract COVID-19 without taking safety measures as drastic as this -- so please be careful and follow the governor's orders.

There are a lot of great resources out there, including: [LA County's Department of Public Health's website](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b802/29776869/aeb9104/569fafd9/4268937936/VEsH/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "http://publichealth.lacounty.gov/media/Coronavirus/") [Ventura County Emergency Information website](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b802/29776869/aeb9104/569fafda/4268937936/VEsE/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://www.vcemergency.com/") [California's Coronavirus Response website](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b802/29776869/aeb9104/569fafdb/4268937936/VEsF/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://covid19.ca.gov/") [Centers for Disease Control and Prevention website](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b802/29776869/aeb9104/569fafd4/4268937936/VEsC/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/index.html") **
### 2\. IF YOU THINK YOU HAVE COVID-19
** >From California's Coronavirus Response website: If you think you might have coronavirus, contact your health care provider. Local health departments and health care providers determine who needs testing and hospitalization on a case-by-case basis.

Seek treatment by calling your doctor for a phone evaluation if:
* You have difficulty breathing (shortness of breath)
* You feel like symptoms (such as fever and cough) are getting worse rapidly
* You are unable to care for symptoms at home

| [](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b802/29776869/aeb9104/569fafd5/4268937936/VEsD/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "http://publichealth.lacounty.gov/media/Coronavirus/CoronavirusInfographicEnglish.pdf")
---

**If you need to go to the hospital, call ahead so they can prepare for your arrival.** If you need to call 911, tell the 911 operator you're experiencing coronavirus symptoms so the ambulance provider can prepare to treat you safely.

**
### 3\. SOCIAL DISTANCING MENTAL HEALTH TIPS
** LA County's Department of Public Health put together this guide on how to cope during this emotionally stressful time:

1\. Manage Your Stress
* Maintain a routine.
* Make time to relax and rest.

2\. Be Informed and Inform Your Family
* Become familiar with local medical and mental health resources in your community.
* Give honest, age-appropriate information to children and remember to stay calm; children often feel what you feel.

3\. Connect with Your Community online or through the phone
* Keep contact with family and friends through social messaging or through phone calls.
* Reach out to neighbors and friends with special needs who may need your help.

4\. Reach Out and Help while maintaining necessary social distancing guidelines
* If you know someone affected by the outbreak, call them to see how they are doing, and remember to keep their confidentiality.
* Consider an act of kindness for those who have been asked to practice social distancing, such as having a meal delivered.

5\. Be Sensitive
* Avoid blaming anyone or assuming someone has the disease because of the way they look or where they or their families come from.
* An infectious disease is not connected to any racial or ethnic group; speak up in kindness when you hear false rumors or negative stereotypes that foster racism and xenophobia.

You can also always contact our campaign at [info@christyforcongress.org](mailto:info@christyforcongress.org) if you have anything you need our help with.

Stay safe, continue social distancing, and together, we can get through this.

\-- Christy

I want to share some resources and information about coronavirus -- from symptoms to ways to mitigate the spread of the disease, this is all good to know while we are all working on getting through this. 1\. IF YOU LIVE IN CALIFORNIA Governor Gavin Newsom has called for all Californians to remain indoors except for absolutely necessary reasons like a trip to the pharmacy, a visit to a doctor, or a run to the grocery store to stock up on food. Folks are safer at home, and by practicing social distancing, we can mitigate the spread of coronavirus. Projections show that more than 50% of Californians could contract COVID-19 without taking safety measures as drastic as this -- so please be careful and follow the governor's orders. There are a lot of great resources out there, including: "" "" "" "" 2\. IF YOU THINK YOU HAVE COVID-19 >From California's Coronavirus Response website: If you think you might have coronavirus, contact your health care provider. Local health departments and health care providers determine who needs testing and hospitalization on a case-by-case basis. Seek treatment by calling your doctor for a phone evaluation if: You have difficulty breathing (shortness of breath) You feel like symptoms (such as fever and cough) are getting worse rapidly You are unable to care for symptoms at home | "" If you need to go to the hospital, call ahead so they can prepare for your arrival. If you need to call 911, tell the 911 operator you're experiencing coronavirus symptoms so the ambulance provider can prepare to treat you safely. 3\. SOCIAL DISTANCING MENTAL HEALTH TIPS LA County's Department of Public Health put together this guide on how to cope during this emotionally stressful time: 1\. Manage Your Stress Maintain a routine. Make time to relax and rest. 2\. Be Informed and Inform Your Family Become familiar with local medical and mental health resources in your community. Give honest, age-appropriate information to children and remember to stay calm; children often feel what you feel. 3\. Connect with Your Community online or through the phone Keep contact with family and friends through social messaging or through phone calls. Reach out to neighbors and friends with special needs who may need your help. 4\. Reach Out and Help while maintaining necessary social distancing guidelines If you know someone affected by the outbreak, call them to see how they are doing, and remember to keep their confidentiality. Consider an act of kindness for those who have been asked to practice social distancing, such as having a meal delivered. 5\. Be Sensitive Avoid blaming anyone or assuming someone has the disease because of the way they look or where they or their families come from. An infectious disease is not connected to any racial or ethnic group; speak up in kindness when you hear false rumors or negative stereotypes that foster racism and xenophobia. You can also always contact our campaign at mailto:info@christyforcongress.org if you have anything you need our help with. Stay safe, continue social distancing, and together, we can get through this. \-- Christy | | Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress. ---|--- |
